issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead

Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Not targeting the latest versions of Android; compatibility modes apply. Consider testing and updating this version. Consult the `android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES` javadoc for details.
Attribute `usesPermissionFlags` is only used in API level 31 and higher (current min is 26)
Attribute `usesPermissionFlags` is only used in API level 31 and higher (current min is 26)
Attribute `localeConfig` is only used in API level 33 and higher (current min is 26)
issue.errorLine1: <application android:name=".PetitApplication" android:allowBackup="false" android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher" android:label="@string/app_name" android:localeConfig="@xml/locales_config" android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/network_security_config" android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round" android:supportsRtl="true" android:theme="@style/Theme.Petit">

issue.message: If you need access to FINE location, you must request both `ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION` and `ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION`
A newer version of Gradle than 9.4.1 is available: 9.6.1
A newer version of com.android.application than 9.1.0 is available: 9.3.0. (There is also a newer version of 9.1.𝑥 available, if upgrading to 9.3.0 is difficult: 9.1.1)

For locale "es" (Spanish) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "pt" (Portuguese) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "es" (Spanish) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "pt" (Portuguese) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`

Formatting %d followed by words ("days"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("records"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("record"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("month"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("months"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("year"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string

Formatting %d followed by words ("days"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string

Formatting %d followed by words ("days"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("characters"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("characters"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("events"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("added"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string

The attribute `android:allowBackup` is deprecated from Android 12 and higher and may be removed in future versions. Consider adding the attribute `android:dataExtractionRules` specifying an `@xml` resource which configures cloud backups and device transfers on Android 12 and higher.
issue.errorLine1: <application android:name=".PetitApplication" android:allowBackup="false" android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher" android:label="@string/app_name" android:localeConfig="@xml/locales_config" android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/network_security_config" android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round" android:supportsRtl="true" android:theme="@style/Theme.Petit">

issue.message: This folder configuration (`v26`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 26. Merge all the resources in this folder into `mipmap-anydpi`.
